summary: Both prior CHANGES sites are now fully covered. (1) hook.go Names: Scope L202-203 + Req-1 L122-124 name internal/hook/hook.go's Names slice (remove 'pre-complete') AND TestNames_Sorted; verified Names=[pre-commit,pre-complete,session-start] so removing the middle entry keeps it sorted (test still passes) and a dedicated AC (spec L253) pins grep+TestNames_Sorted. (2) Scenario REMOVE surface is COMPLETE: grep across the whole tree shows exactly four live-code sites for ScenarioPanelGateBlocksPrematureComplete/scenario_panel_gate/assertPanelGateBlocksIncomplete — the scenario file, its _test.go, the scenario.go L64 registration, and the helper — ALL named in Scope L212-222 (delete both files + remove the L64 registration). The helper assertPanelGateBlocksIncomplete AND the file-local helpers gitOutMust + writeIncompletePanel are used ONLY by scenario_panel_gate.go (verified by grep), so they vanish with the file — no shared-helper dangle. The second test in scenario_panel_gate_test.go, TestAllScenariosRegistersPanelGate (the scenario-count/registry pin), is removed by the file deletion the spec already mandates; and the separate TestAllScenariosRegistersContractHardening map does NOT enumerate panel_gate_blocks_premature_complete, so no count assertion dangles. No new dangling ref introduced. (3) REMOVE justified: the 8 TestPanelGate_* hermetic tests exist in internal/complete/panel_gate_e2e_test.go and genuinely assert the block — TestPanelGate_SubThreshold_Blocks asserts non-zero exit + 'git merge bead/'+beadID fence + guard.HasFinalRecoveryLine, BlockNeverNamesSkipVar pins HC-7, SharedDecisionPin pins the shared internal/panel decision body verbatim. internal/complete does NOT import internal/hook (verified: zero imports), so retiring the hook matcher cannot affect them — unique authoritative-gate coverage is NOT lost; only the LLM behavioral-envelope half is, which the spec explicitly acknowledges as acceptable. NON-BLOCKING residue (acceptable staleness, not removal-surface gaps): conceptual 'pre-complete hook' prose comments in internal/panel/{panel,tally}.go, internal/instruct/panelstate.go, internal/config/config.go (PanelGate toggle is RETAINED for the in-binary gate, so its comment stays accurate); and a historical enumeration of the scenario name in ADR-0037 L270 + specs 093/099 records — these document past work, are not live references, and do not compile/test-break. Worth a one-line touch when R1 amends ADR-0037, but not blocking. Baseline go build ./... is green. APPROVE.
